# Monk Class Features — Player Core 2 (Remaster)

Source: Player Core 2 pg. 114
Class: Monk
Key Attribute: Strength or Dexterity
Hit Points: 10 plus your Constitution modifier (per level)

---

## Class Progression Table

| Your Level | Class Features |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Ancestry and background, attribute boosts, initial proficiencies, flurry of blows, monk feat, powerful fist |
| 2 | Monk feat, skill feat |
| 3 | General feat, incredible movement +10 feet, mystic strikes, skill increase |
| 4 | Monk feat, skill feat |
| 5 | Attribute boosts, ancestry feat, expert strikes, perception expertise, skill increase |
| 6 | Monk feat, skill feat |
| 7 | General feat, incredible movement +15 feet, path to perfection, skill increase, weapon specialization |
| 8 | Monk feat, skill feat |
| 9 | Ancestry feat, metal strikes, monk expertise, skill increase |
| 10 | Attribute boosts, monk feat, skill feat |
| 11 | General feat, incredible movement +20 feet, second path to perfection, skill increase |
| 12 | Monk feat, skill feat |
| 13 | Ancestry feat, graceful mastery, master strikes, skill increase |
| 14 | Monk feat, skill feat |
| 15 | Attribute boosts, general feat, greater weapon specialization, incredible movement +25 feet, skill increase, third path to perfection |
| 16 | Monk feat, skill feat |
| 17 | Adamantine strikes, ancestry feat, graceful legend, skill increase |
| 18 | Monk feat, skill feat |
| 19 | General feat, incredible movement +30 feet, perfected form, skill increase |
| 20 | Attribute boosts, monk feat, skill feat |

---

## Initial Proficiencies

At 1st level, you gain the listed proficiency ranks in the following statistics. You are untrained in anything not listed unless you gain a better proficiency rank in some other way.

### Perception
- Trained in Perception

### Saving Throws
- Expert in Fortitude
- Expert in Reflex
- Expert in Will

### Skills
- Trained in a number of skills equal to 4 plus your Intelligence modifier

### Attacks
- Trained in simple weapons
- Trained in unarmed attacks

### Defenses
- Untrained in all armor
- Expert in unarmored defense

### Class DC
- Trained in monk class DC

---

## Key Terms

**Flourish:** Actions with this trait are special techniques that require too much exertion for you to perform frequently. You can use only 1 action with the flourish trait per turn.

**Incapacitation:** An ability with this trait can take a character out of the fight. But when you use an incapacitation effect against a creature of higher level than you, you reduce the degree of success of your attack roll by one step, and that creature improves the degree of success of its saving throws for that effect by one step.

**Qi Spells:** By tapping into an inner reserve of energy, called qi, you can cast special spells called qi spells, which are a type of focus spell. It costs 1 Focus Point to cast a focus spell. When you gain your first qi spell, you decide whether your qi spells are divine or occult spells, and you become trained in spell attack modifiers and spell DCs; your key spellcasting attribute is Wisdom. You also gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. You refill your focus pool during your daily preparations, and you regain 1 Focus Point by spending 10 minutes using the Refocus activity to steady your mind and breath to draw in more qi from the world around you. Focus spells are automatically heightened to half your level rounded up. The maximum Focus Points your focus pool can hold is equal to the number of focus spells you have, but it can never be more than 3 points.

**Stance:** A stance is a general combat strategy that you enter by using an action with the stance trait, and that you remain in for some time. A stance lasts until you get knocked out, until its requirements (if any) are violated, until the encounter ends, or until you use a stance action again, whichever comes first. After you take an action that has the stance trait, you can't take another one for 1 round. You can enter or be in a stance only in encounter mode. You can Dismiss a stance.

**Venomous:** These weapons inject poison into every hit. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it deals an additional 1 persistent poison damage. This increases to 2 persistent poison damage if the weapon has a greater striking rune.

---

## Level 1 Class Features

### Ancestry and Background
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** In addition to the abilities provided by your class at 1st level, you have the benefits of your selected ancestry and background.

### Attribute Boosts
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** In addition to what you get from your class at 1st level, you have four free boosts to different attribute modifiers. At 5th level and every 5 levels thereafter, you get four free boosts to different attribute modifiers. If an attribute modifier is already +4 or higher, it takes two boosts to increase it; you get a partial boost and must boost that attribute again at a later level to increase it by 1.

### Initial Proficiencies
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 1st level, you gain a number of proficiencies that represent your basic training, noted at the start of this class.

### Flurry of Blows
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** 1 action [one-action]
- **Traits:** Flourish, Monk
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 116
- **Description:** You can attack rapidly with the Flurry of Blows action. Make two unarmed Strikes. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses. Apply your multiple attack penalty to the Strikes normally. As a flourish ability, you can use Flurry of Blows only once per turn.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is one of the most action-efficient abilities in the game — two Strikes for a single action. The flourish trait limits it to once per turn. Both Strikes use the same MAP progression (e.g., first Strike at current MAP, second Strike at MAP-5 or MAP-4 with agile). Combining damage for resistances/weaknesses means if each Strike does 5 damage vs a creature with resistance 5, the combined 10 damage is reduced by 5 (not each individually).

### Monk Feats
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 1st level and every even-numbered level thereafter, you gain a monk class feat.

### Powerful Fist
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** Your fists are deadly weapons. The damage die for your fist increases to 1d6 instead of 1d4. You don't take the normal –2 circumstance penalty when making a lethal attack with your fist or any other unarmed attacks.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This upgrades the base fist damage die from 1d4 to 1d6 and removes the nonlethal-to-lethal penalty. This is a core feature that makes monks viable unarmed combatants from level 1.

---

## Level 2 Class Features

### Skill Feats
- **Level:** 2
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 2nd level and every 2 levels thereafter, you gain a skill feat. You must be trained or better in the corresponding skill to select a skill feat.

---

## Level 3 Class Features

### General Feats
- **Level:** 3
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 3rd level and every 4 levels thereafter, you gain a general feat.

### Incredible Movement
- **Level:** 3
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You move like the wind. You gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed whenever you're not wearing armor. The bonus increases by 5 feet for every 4 levels you have beyond 3rd.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is a status bonus, so it doesn't stack with other status bonuses to Speed. Progression: +10 feet at level 3, +15 feet at level 7, +20 feet at level 11, +25 feet at level 15, +30 feet at level 19. Requires no armor (not even light armor). Unarmored defense is the monk's primary defense anyway.

### Mystic Strikes
- **Level:** 3
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** Focusing your will into your physical attacks imbues them with mystical energy. Your unarmed attacks become magical, allowing them to get past resistances to non-magical attacks. However, you still need an item such as handwraps of mighty blows to gain an item bonus to attack rolls or increase your attacks' weapon damage dice.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Makes unarmed attacks count as magical for overcoming resistances. Does NOT grant an item bonus or property rune effects — you still need handwraps of mighty blows for those.

### Skill Increases
- **Level:** 3
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 3rd level and every 2 levels thereafter, you gain a skill increase. You can use an increase to either become trained in one skill you're untrained in, or to increase your proficiency rank in one skill in which you're already trained to expert. At 7th level, you can use skill increases to become a master in a skill in which you're already an expert, and at 15th level, you can use them to become legendary in a skill in which you're already a master.

---

## Level 5 Class Features

### Ancestry Feats
- **Level:** 5
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** In addition to the ancestry feat you started with, you gain an ancestry feat at 5th level and every 4 levels thereafter.

### Expert Strikes
- **Level:** 5
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You've practiced martial arts and have now surpassed your former skill. Your proficiency ranks for unarmed attacks and simple weapons increase to expert. When you get a critical hit with an unarmed attack in the brawling group, you get its critical specialization effect.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Brawling critical specialization effect: The target must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or be slowed 1 until the end of your next turn. This makes critical hits with fists and most monk unarmed attacks (which are in the brawling group) significantly more powerful.

### Perception Expertise
- **Level:** 5
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You remain alert to threats around you. Your proficiency rank for Perception increases to expert.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is a shared feature. Monks get Perception expertise at level 5, which is earlier than some classes but later than martials like Fighter (who starts expert) or Ranger (who starts expert).

---

## Level 7 Class Features

### Path to Perfection
- **Level:** 7
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You have progressed along your own path to enlightenment. Choose your Fortitude, Reflex, or Will saving throw. Your proficiency rank for the chosen saving throw increases to master. When you roll a success on the chosen saving throw, you get a critical success instead.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is one of three "Path to Perfection" features (7th, 11th, 15th). The monk starts with expert in all three saves, making them extremely well-rounded defensively. The success-to-critical-success upgrade is exceptionally powerful. Choose based on what threats you expect — Fortitude for poisons/diseases, Reflex for area damage, Will for mental effects.

### Weapon Specialization (Shared Feature)
- **Level:** 7
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You've learned how to inflict greater injuries with the weapons you know best. You deal 2 additional damage with weapons and unarmed attacks in which you are an expert. This damage increases to 3 if you're a master, and 4 if you're legendary.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is a shared class feature that appears on many classes. The flat damage bonus applies per Strike, making it particularly valuable with Flurry of Blows (applied to each of the two Strikes). Damage scales with proficiency: +2 (expert), +3 (master), +4 (legendary).

---

## Level 9 Class Features

### Metal Strikes
- **Level:** 9
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You can adjust your body to strike with the mystic energy of rare metals. Your unarmed attacks are treated as cold iron and silver. This allows you to deal more damage to a variety of supernatural creatures, such as demons, devils, and fey.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Cold iron bypasses weaknesses of fey and some other creatures. Silver bypasses weaknesses of devils, werecreatures, and other fiends. This makes monks highly effective against a wide range of supernatural enemies without needing special equipment.

### Monk Expertise
- **Level:** 9
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** Your proficiency rank for your monk class DC increases to expert. If you have qi spells, your proficiency rank for spell attacks and spell DCs increases to expert.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This improves the DC for monk abilities like the brawling critical specialization effect (from Expert Strikes) and any class feat abilities that call for saves against your class DC. If you've taken qi spell feats, this also boosts your spell attack and DC.

---

## Level 11 Class Features

### Second Path to Perfection
- **Level:** 11
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You've learned to find perfection in every success. Choose a different saving throw than the one you chose for your path to perfection. Your proficiency rank for the chosen saving throw increases to master. If you roll a success with the chosen saving throw, you instead critically succeed.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is the second of three Path to Perfection features. You must choose a different save than the one chosen at level 7. After this, two of your three saves have master proficiency with success-to-crit-success upgrades. The monk is now one of the hardest classes to affect with save-based abilities.

---

## Level 13 Class Features

### Graceful Mastery
- **Level:** 13
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You move with perpetual grace, turning aside blows. Your proficiency rank for unarmored defense increases to master.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This upgrades unarmored defense from expert to master. Combined with a high Dexterity, this gives the monk an AC comparable to or exceeding heavy-armored characters. The monk at this point has master unarmored defense, making them very hard to hit.

### Master Strikes
- **Level:** 13
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You've honed your body into an even deadlier weapon. Your proficiency ranks for unarmed attacks and simple weapons increase to master.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This brings unarmed attack proficiency to master, matching Fighter weapon proficiency at this level. Combined with weapon specialization (which now gives +3 damage at master), the monk's damage output increases significantly. Also improves the likelihood of critical hits.

---

## Level 15 Class Features

### Greater Weapon Specialization (Shared Feature)
- **Level:** 15
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** Your damage from weapon specialization increases to 4 with weapons and unarmed attacks in which you're an expert, 6 if you're a master, and 8 if you're legendary.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is a shared class feature. At this level, monks have master proficiency in unarmed attacks, so they deal +6 damage per Strike from this feature. With Flurry of Blows making two Strikes per action, this adds +12 damage per Flurry of Blows.

### Third Path to Perfection
- **Level:** 15
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You have made great progress in your personal studies of enlightenment. Choose one of the saving throws you selected for path to perfection or second path to perfection. Your proficiency rank for the chosen type of save increases to legendary. When you roll a critical failure on the chosen type of save, you get a failure instead. When you roll a failure on the chosen type of save against an effect that deals damage, you take half damage.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This upgrades one of your already-master saves to legendary, and gives two additional defensive benefits: critical failure becomes failure, and failure against damaging effects means half damage. The remaining save (the one not chosen at 7 or 11) stays at expert. After this, the monk has legendary/master/expert saves, making them arguably the most resilient class against save-based effects in the game.

---

## Level 17 Class Features

### Adamantine Strikes
- **Level:** 17
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** When you focus your will into your limbs, your blows are as unyielding as the hardest of metals. Your unarmed attacks are treated as adamantine.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Adamantine bypasses hardness of most objects and constructs, and overcomes the weaknesses of certain high-level creatures. Combined with cold iron and silver from Metal Strikes at level 9, the monk's unarmed attacks now count as cold iron, silver, AND adamantine simultaneously.

### Graceful Legend
- **Level:** 17
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** Your sublime movement grants you unparalleled protection and offense. Your proficiency rank for unarmored defense increases to legendary, and your proficiency rank for your monk class DC increases to master. If you have qi spells, your proficiency rank for spell attack modifiers and spell DCs increases to master.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Legendary unarmored defense is the highest possible AC proficiency, matching only the Fighter's legendary weapon proficiency. This makes the monk one of the highest-AC classes in the game at this level. The class DC increase to master improves all monk abilities that use the class DC. Qi spells also get master proficiency if applicable.

---

## Level 19 Class Features

### Perfected Form
- **Level:** 19
- **Action Cost:** Passive (fortune effect)
- **Traits:** Fortune
- **Description:** You have purged incompetence from your techniques. On your first Strike of your turn, if you roll lower than 10, you can treat the attack roll as a 10. This is a fortune effect.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This effectively gives you a minimum roll of 10 on your first Strike each turn (similar to the "take 10" concept). As a fortune effect, it doesn't stack with other fortune effects. With master proficiency in unarmed attacks at this level, a minimum roll of 10 means your first Strike almost always hits and frequently crits. This applies to the first Strike of Flurry of Blows if that's your first attack action.

---

## Summary of Shared Features Used by Monk

| Shared Feature | Level Gained | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ancestry and Background | 1 | Standard for all classes |
| Attribute Boosts | 1, 5, 10, 15, 20 | Standard for all classes |
| Initial Proficiencies | 1 | Monk-specific proficiency set |
| Skill Feats | 2 (every 2 levels) | Standard for most classes |
| General Feats | 3 (every 4 levels) | Standard for all classes |
| Skill Increases | 3 (every 2 levels) | Standard for all classes |
| Ancestry Feats | 5 (every 4 levels) | Standard for all classes |
| Weapon Specialization | 7 | Shared feature (+2/+3/+4 damage) |
| Greater Weapon Specialization | 15 | Shared feature (+4/+6/+8 damage) |

---

## Proficiency Progression Summary

### Unarmed Attacks / Simple Weapons
| Level | Proficiency |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Trained |
| 5 | Expert (Expert Strikes) |
| 13 | Master (Master Strikes) |

### Unarmored Defense
| Level | Proficiency |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Expert |
| 13 | Master (Graceful Mastery) |
| 17 | Legendary (Graceful Legend) |

### Perception
| Level | Proficiency |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Trained |
| 5 | Expert (Perception Expertise) |

### Monk Class DC
| Level | Proficiency |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Trained |
| 9 | Expert (Monk Expertise) |
| 17 | Master (Graceful Legend) |

### Saving Throws
| Level | Fortitude | Reflex | Will |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Expert | Expert | Expert |
| 7 | Master* | Master* | Master* |
| 11 | Master* | Master* | Master* |
| 15 | Legendary** | Legendary** | Legendary** |

\* Path to Perfection (level 7) and Second Path to Perfection (level 11) each let you choose one save to increase to master. You choose two different saves total.

\*\* Third Path to Perfection (level 15) upgrades one of the two saves you already chose to legendary. The third save remains at expert.

### Qi Spells (if applicable)
| Level | Proficiency |
| --- | --- |
| When first gained | Trained |
| 9 | Expert (Monk Expertise) |
| 17 | Master (Graceful Legend) |

## Incredible Movement Progression

| Level | Speed Bonus |
| --- | --- |
| 3 | +10 feet |
| 7 | +15 feet |
| 11 | +20 feet |
| 15 | +25 feet |
| 19 | +30 feet |

Status bonus. Requires no armor worn.

---

## Unarmed Strike Material Progression

| Level | Material Properties |
| --- | --- |
| 3 | Magical (Mystic Strikes) |
| 9 | Cold iron and silver (Metal Strikes) |
| 17 | Adamantine (Adamantine Strikes) |

All material properties are cumulative — at level 17, unarmed attacks count as magical, cold iron, silver, AND adamantine.
